steps.title: Use the infinite-solution condition
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has infinitely many solutions only when both equations describe the same line.

steps.title: Rewrite the first equation
steps.content:
Multiply the first equation by $4$ so the coefficient of $y$ is $1$:
$$4\cdot\frac{2}{5}x+4\cdot\frac{1}{4}y=4\cdot\frac{3}{8}$$
$$\frac{8}{5}x+y=\frac{3}{2}$$

steps.title: Match the coefficients
steps.content: The second equation is $ax+y=c$. To describe the same line, the coefficient of $x$ must be $\frac{8}{5}$. The answer is D.
